title: ranunculus acrophilus
intro: Ranunculus acrophilus, also known as alpine buttercup, is a perennial plant that belongs to the Ranunculaceae family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and alkaloids. The health benefits of this plant include reducing inflammation and relieving symptoms associated with respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. Ranunculus acrophilus can be used to make herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
